title: verbascum × bastardii
intro: Verbascum × bastardii, also known as Bastard's Cabbage or Mullein Vervain, is a hybrid plant species that belongs to the Verbascum genus, combining characteristics of Verbascum thapsus and Verbascum lychnitis. Traditionally, Verbascum × bastardii has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant qualities, which can help alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant's health benefits include its potential to reduce fever, soothe skin irritations, and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, Verbascum × bastardii is often used to make infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be applied topically or ingested as a tea to harness its medicinal properties.
